at.jku.risc.stout.urau.data

Class InputParser<T extends Equation>

  • java.lang.Object
    • at.jku.risc.stout.urau.data.InputParser<T>

  • public class InputParser<T extends Equation>
extends Object
    Tiny term parser with the following rules:
    • [A-Za-z1-9_.] are allowed for variable names and function names. Names can be of any length.
    • If the name is followed by an opening parenthesis a Function will be generated. Parameter inside the pair of parenthesis are wrapped in a Hedge
    • Else if the first letter of the name is within [ VARIABLE_START, z] an individual variable will be generated.
      • If the variable name starts with an upper case letter it will become a hedge variable. Otherwise an individual variable will be instantiates.
    • Else a Function with zero parameters (=constant) will be generated.
    Author:
    Alexander Baumgartner

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static char CLOSING_PARENTHESIS 
      static char OPENING_PARENTHESIS 
      static char VARIABLE_START
      default = 'u' => A word starting with 'u-z' will become a variable.
      VARIABLE_START has to be a lower case letter! Hedge variables are denoted by upper case letters.

        default = 'u' => A word starting with 'u-z' will become a variable.
        VARIABLE_START has to be a lower case letter! Hedge variables are denoted by upper case letters.

      • isNameChar

      • parseEquationSystem

        public void parseEquationSystem(String unifProblem,
                       PrintStream debug)
                         throws IOException,
                                MalformedTermException
        Parse the given input string and add all equations to the system. Use parseHedgeEquation(Reader, Reader) if possible! It performs better and saves space by reading directly from the data streams;)
        Throws:
        IOException
        MalformedTermException

      • parseHedgeEquation

        public void parseHedgeEquation(Reader leftHedge,
                      Reader rightHedge)
                        throws IOException,
                               MalformedTermException
        A hedge equation has the form:
        (s1, s2,...) =^= (t1, t2,...)
        Parameters:
        leftHedge - The left sequence (s1, s2,...)
        rightHedge - The right sequence (t1, t2,...)
        Throws:
        IOException
        MalformedTermException

      • parseTerm

        public TermNode parseTerm(Reader in)
                   throws IOException,
                          MalformedTermException
        Throws:
        IOException
        MalformedTermException
